title: malus sorbus
intro: Malus sorbus, also known as Rowan or Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its rich content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Malus sorbus have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Malus sorbus include teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to skin conditions and wounds.
